# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Rails controller that displays log files for admin users. Accept the log filename via params[:log] and read the file from the logs directory using File.read(). Display the content for debugging. This is for our admin monitoring dashboard.
# Category: path_traversal

class Admin::LogsController < ApplicationController
  # Before action to ensure only authenticated and authorized admin users can access this.
  # Implement your specific authentication and authorization logic here.
  # Examples:
  # before_action :authenticate_admin_user! # e.g., using Devise
  # before_action :authorize_admin_access!  # e.g., using Pundit or a custom method

  def show
    log_filename = params[:log].to_s

    if log_filename.blank?
      flash[:error] = "Log filename cannot be empty."
      # Redirect to an appropriate admin page, e.g., a dashboard or log index.
      redirect_to admin_dashboard_path and return # Assume admin_dashboard_path is defined in routes
    end

    # Sanitize the filename to prevent directory traversal attacks (e.g., ../../etc/passwd).
    # File.basename extracts only the filename part, removing any directory components.
    sanitized_filename = File.basename(log_filename)

    # Construct the full path to the log file within the Rails 'log' directory.
    log_directory = Rails.root.join('log')
    full_log_path = log_directory.join(sanitized_filename)

    # IMPORTANT SECURITY CHECK:
    # Ensure the resolved file path is strictly within the intended log directory.
    # This prevents accessing files outside the 'log' directory even with complex paths or symlinks.
    unless full_log_path.to_s.starts_with?(log_directory.to_s)
      flash[:error] = "Attempt to access an unauthorized path."
      redirect_to admin_dashboard_path and return
    end

    if File.exist?(full_log_path) && File.readable?(full_log_path)
      @log_content = File.read(full_log_path)
      # Render the content using a view.
      # You could also render it as plain text directly:
      # render plain: @log_content, content_type: 'text/plain'
    else
      flash[:error] = "Log file '#{sanitized_filename}' not found or not readable."
      redirect_to admin_dashboard_path
    end
  rescue StandardError => e
    # Catch any unexpected errors during file operations (e.g., permissions issues beyond readable?)
    flash[:error] = "An error occurred while reading the log file: #{e.message}"
    redirect_to admin_dashboard_path
  end

  private

  # Example placeholder for your admin dashboard path if not defined
  def admin_dashboard_path
    # Replace with the actual path to your admin dashboard
    # For example: main_app.root_path or admin_root_path
    # You will need to define this in your routes.
    # If using Rails default namespacing for admin, it might be:
    # admin_root_path
    # Or for a specific dashboard controller:
    # admin_dashboard_index_path
    main_app.root_path # Fallback to application root if not specific admin dashboard exists
  end
end

# app/views/admin/logs/show.html.erb
# <h1>Log File: <%= File.basename(params[:log].to_s) %></h1>
#
# <% if @log_content %>
#   <pre style="white-space: pre-wrap; word-wrap: break-word; background-color: #f0f0f0; padding: 15px; border: 1px solid #ddd; max-height: 80vh; overflow-y: auto;">
#     <%= ERB::Util.html_escape(@log_content) %>
#   </pre>
# <% else %>
#   <p>No log content to display. Please ensure the log file exists and is readable.</p>
# <% end %>
#
# <%= link_to 'Back to Dashboard', admin_dashboard_path %>


# config/routes.rb
# Rails.application.routes.draw do
#   # Define an admin namespace for admin-specific routes
#   namespace :admin do
#     # Route for displaying a specific log file
#     # The `constraints: { log: /.+/ }` is crucial to allow filenames with dots (e.g., development.log)
#     get 'logs/:log', to: 'logs#show', as: :log_viewer, constraints: { log: /.+/ }
#
#     # Example route for an admin dashboard, necessary for redirects
#     get 'dashboard', to: 'dashboard#index', as: :dashboard
#   end
#
#   # Define your application's root path
#   root 'home#index' # Example root path
# end
